response:
"Let the total count of numbers be denoted by n.
  midnight marks the start of our second thought process here.
  We know the five largest sum to twenty nine percent of the total.
  The five smallest sum to twenty six percent of the total sum.
  These two groups might overlap if the total count is small enough.
  If n equals nine then the largest and smallest sets share one number.
  The sum of all numbers must be greater than the sum of any subset.
  Calculating the overlap reveals that nine is the only valid solution.
  Thus the board holds exactly nine positive numbers in total."
